title: OpenAI will delay GPT-5.6 after Trump administration request
slides: OpenAI delays GPT-5.6. What did Washington request?
The request came from the Trump administration last week.
The delay isn’t about safety—it’s about export controls.
Your competitors might release models while you wait.
Test your current pipeline now for regulatory gaps.
thread_text:
1. Delay by request?

The Trump administration asked OpenAI to hold off on GPT-5.6 last week.

2. Not about safety.

Export controls, not model risks, are the reason behind the delay.

3. Competitors keep moving.

While you wait, rivals may ship their own models unhindered.

4. Check your pipeline now.

Regulatory gaps in your current setup could catch you off guard.

5. Simulate a compliance test.

Take your latest model and run it through current export rules this week.

script: OpenAI delayed GPT-5.6 after Washington asked.
It was the Trump administration last week.
The reason is export controls, not safety.
Your competitors may launch while you wait.
Test your pipeline for regulatory gaps now.

instagram.caption:
The Trump administration directly asked OpenAI to hold off launching GPT-5.6 last week.

This isn't about safety fears. It's about export controls—Washington wants to tighten what AI tech leaves the country. Your competitors aren't under the same hold.

If you build with or against GPT models, your timeline just shifted. A rival's unregulated model could hit the market first.

Run your current pipeline through the latest export rules this week. What blocker would catch you off guard? ⏳🔍

Your competitors may release unhindered models during OpenAI's regulatory pause on GPT-5.6.
